editorial.headline: Benintendi Homer Lifts White Sox Into First Place
editorial.subheadline: CWS 5, NYY 1. Top of the division changes hands — or firms up.
editorial.lead_angle:
Andrew Benintendi sent a Fernando Cruz fastball deep into the right field seats, silencing Yankee Stadium and seizing first place for the Chicago White Sox. The eighth-inning grand slam broke a 1-1 deadlock and powered the visitors to a 5-1 victory over the New York Yankees. For seven innings, the two best teams in the division traded zeroes, a solo shot apiece the only marks on the board. Then Benintendi stepped up with the sacks full and cleared them, changing the shape of the pennant race with one swing.

The eighth began as a stalemate. But New York’s bullpen faltered against the heart of the order. Cruz could not find the plate, loading the bases on a walk and two singles before facing Benintendi. The outfielder took one pitch before delivering the final verdict. The Yankees, who had matched Colson Montgomery's second-inning homer with a Ryan McMahon blast in the third, had no answer for the sudden crooked number. The inning turned a tight contest into a declarative statement.

But the man who made Benintendi’s heroics possible was starter Sean Burke. The big right-hander was masterful, working into the eighth and holding the potent Yankees to a single run on their own turf. He scattered just four hits and struck out eight, keeping his team level long enough for the offense to awaken. Burke earned the win, his eighth, while Fernando Cruz took the loss. With the victory, the White Sox put a firm grip on first place in the American League Central.
editorial.reason: Two division leaders, one winner — the standings implications make this the marquee matchup.
